methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kvertus is a growth-stage Defense & Security company that has raised approximately $130M to date. The target operates within a sector containing 21 identified peers, though the narrow peer set for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables is constrained: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. This composition limits traditional peer-median valuation methodology.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage comparable data. A filtered search for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier peers within Defense & Security yielded fewer than three datapoints, the minimum threshold for reliable median analysis. Expanding to all 21 sector peers would introduce heterogeneity that would distort valuation guidance, as companies span materially different stages, business models, and capital intensity profiles.

The available comp universe—while illustrative of the market landscape—does not yet support a specific valuation range via quantitative triangulation. To establish credible valuation guidance, the analysis would require either (1) narrowing to a more homogeneous subset of peers with disclosed metrics, or (2) applying alternative methods such as precedent transaction analysis or discounted cash flow modeling, neither of which is supported by the provided data. The key uncertainty is the scarcity of reliably comparable Defense & Security companies at Kvertus's current stage and scale.
